step_by_step_thinking:
Calcification occurs gradually during tooth development, with different structures completing their mineralization at various stages. The root calcification of primary (deciduous) teeth typically begins around six months to one year after birth and continues until about age four or five when all roots have fully formed and calcified.

The correct option for completion of root calcification in primary teeth is B: 4 years. Clinical Reasoning: 
Deciduous teeth start developing intrauterine but continue mineralization postnatally. Root formation and calcification generally take several years; however, they are largely complete by early childhood, specifically between ages three and five.

Clinical Analysis:
Tooth root calcification progresses from infancy through early childhood, culminating primarily before the child reaches school age. Given this timeline, root calcifications of deciduous teeth are mostly completed by approximately 4 years old.
